author: Debdeep Banerjee
name: 6L Depth Minimalism + U-Net + Sliding Window Eval
blurb: 6-layer transformer (3 encoder + 3 decoder) with U-Net skip connections beats the 9-layer baseline using depth minimalism: untied embeddings, full 4H/4KV attention (no GQA), half batch size (262K), LeakyReLU^2(0.5), learnable residual mixing, seq_len=2048, logit_softcap=12, and sliding window evaluation at stride=64. Demonstrates that 6 layers can match or exceed the 9-layer baseline through careful architectural choices.
calibration_note: Trained on 1xA100 80GB. Baseline step_avg on same hardware: 705.97ms vs 43.54ms on 8xH100 (16.2x ratio). Estimated 8xH100 step_avg: ~19ms, total ~7.9 min well under 10-min cap.
